heading: Condemnation of land; acceptance of donations
label: 242 Condemnation of land; acceptance of donations
text: The Secretary of the Interior is authorized to cause condemnation proceedings to be instituted in the name of the United States under the provisions of , to acquire title to the lands, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto that are privately owned within the boundaries of the said national park, and such property, when acquired, shall become a part thereof: , That when the owner of such lands, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto shall fix a price for the same, which, in the opinion of the Secretary of the Interior, shall be reasonable, the Secretary may purchase the same without further delay: , That the Secretary of the Interior is authorized to accept, on behalf of the United States, donations of land, interests therein, or rights pertaining thereto required for the Theodore Roosevelt National Park: , That title and evidence of title to land and interests therein acquired for said park shall be satisfactory to the Attorney General.section 3113 of title 40ProvidedProvided furtherAnd provided further
notes: Codification“” substituted in text for “the Act of , entitled ‘An Act to authorize the condemnation of lands for sites for public buildings, and other purposes’ ()” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works.Section 3113 of title 4025 Stat. 357Pub.
